results.title: Notes from building Tinkerfont
results.content:
## What Tinkerfont does

Tinkerfont is a browser extension for inspecting, replacing, and testing fonts on live websites.

 Inspect: right-click any text for family, weight, size, color, and contrast. Copy what you need.
 Detect: open the panel and see every font family on the page, with counts.
 Swap: search 1,900+ open fonts from Bunny Fonts, or upload your own `.woff2`, `.woff`, `.ttf`, or `.otf`. Apply in one click.
 Scope: limit a swap to the nav, a hero, or any area you pick on the page.
 Persist: rules save per hostname. Export and import your setup as JSON.

Free on Chrome and Firefox. No account. Everything stays in local storage on your device.

## How the idea grew [...] ## Try it

 Chrome: Chrome Web Store
 Firefox: Firefox Add-ons

If you use it for work, I'd love to hear what breaks or what's missing.

Tagged in tech [...] Use the left or right arrow keys on your keyboard to navigate between posts.

RandomGuestbookStatus

Feed url

Copied to clipboard

# Notes from building Tinkerfont

I'm a generalist. I design, I code, I end up in a lot of places where typography matters but isn't really the job. Reviewing a client's staging site. Onboarding to a codebase I didn't write. Wondering if a page would just read better in a different font face. The question comes up a lot: what's actually being used here, and what would it look like with something else?

DevTools gets you part of the way. But there wasn't a quick way to just try a font on the live page and move on, so Tinkerfont started as a small tool to close that gap.

results.title: Tinkerfont - Chrome Web Store
results.content:
Tinkerfont helps you inspect the fonts currently in use, replace them with fonts from Bunny Fonts or your own uploads, and see the result instantly on live content, real layouts, and actual breakpoints. Perfect for designers, front-end engineers, and tinkerers evaluating typography in context. Features: ✔️ Right-click font inspector to detect fonts used on the current page ✔️ Replace individual fonts or all fonts at once ✔️ Scope changes to specific areas of a page ✔️ Browse 1959 fonts from Bunny Fonts ✔️ Upload and test custom font files (.woff2, .woff, .ttf, .otf) ✔️ Save rules per website hostname ✔️ Import and export settings between browsers ✔️ Works on many CSP-restricted sites via inlined fonts ✔️ Automatically re-applies changes on SPAs and dynamic pages ✔️ Light and dark mode [...] Chrome Web Store logoChrome Web Store

 My extensions & themes
 Appearance
 Developer Dashboard
 Give feedback

Sign in

## Overview

The fastest way to inspect, replace, and test new fonts on live websites. [...] changes on SPAs and dynamic pages ✔️ Light and dark mode support ✔️ Copy font file URLs (experimental) Common uses: ✔️ Inspect and copy font specs from any site without DevTools ✔️ Test brand fonts on staging or production sites ✔️ Evaluate typography on real dashboards, tables, forms, and content ✔️ Improve readability on websites you visit regularly Get started guide: ✔️  No DevTools. No temporary CSS edits. No mockups. Just typography experimentation on the live web. ♻️ Version History 1.0.2 — Custom tab UX: display name and save appear after choosing a file; cleaner saved-fonts list layout. Updated Bunny Fonts catalog. Reduced the size of package. 1.0.1 — Right-click inspector fixes: reliable copy, improved font file detection, clearer menu labels. 1.0.0 — Launch.

results.title: Font Playground - Source Foundry
results.content:
## Font playground

See Hack (and other monospaced fonts) live in action!   
 Install your fonts locally and they will show up in the dropdown selections.   
 Use the dropdown options to modify the typeface, text size, language, and highlighter scheme.

Problems with the Font Playground? Issue reporting is available here.

Hack 

A typeface designed for source code.
